summary: sensivity ships Windows persistence + obfuscated remote-control backdoor masquerading as OneDrive
details: On import or CLI start, launcher.js reads server.obf.js (a ~178KB obfuscator.io / RC4 string-array payload) and executes it via eval(). The package then installs Windows boot persistence by writing HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\OneDriveUpdate to launch wscript.exe against a shipped OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs that re-runs `node launcher.js` at every login — disguised as a Microsoft OneDrive updater, with windowsHide and a 'Runtime Broker' process title. A detached supervisor (SENSIVITY_SUPERVISOR=1) respawns a worker that boots an Express + socket.io server bound to 0.0.0.0:3000, exposing unauthenticated control endpoints (/api/trigger/start, /api/kill, /api/qr, /api/status) and socket events (startCheat, killSensivity, configSync, performAction) to anyone reachable on the network. The package also ships sens.node, a 6.8MB Windows native binary containing FiveM/GTA cheat strings (Freecam, Teleport, GTAProcess, FiveMProcess, aimbot bone names) — unrelated to the package.json description of 'Sensivity Control Panel'. server.obf.js generates a machine fingerprint (HKLM MachineGuid + hostname + C: volume serial, SHA-256 hashed) and POSTs it to a hardcoded remote license endpoint. launcher.js sanitizes log output to rename 'sens.node' → 'program module' and strip the install path — anti-forensics. Every behavior fires automatically on `require()` or running the bin; no user opt-in is required.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
